text.title: Spike Protocol Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Spike Protocol Template application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Time-box ≤ 5 days. Beyond 5 days = epic, not spike. Time-box stated in the ticket.
rule.rationale: Unboxed spikes balloon; 5-day ceiling preserves spike character (exploration, not delivery).

rule.statement: Exit criteria listed up front: '≥3 options evaluated', 'PoC running locally', 'risk assessment complete'. Not 'feel done'.
rule.rationale: Without exit criteria, spikes become busywork; explicit criteria force scope discipline.

rule.statement: Spike produces investigation artefacts (notes, PoC code, decision recommendations). Not production-ready code.
rule.rationale: Spike code in production = unmaintained, untested code in critical paths; explicit no-production rule prevents.

rule.statement: Outcome is published (1-page doc, RFC, ADR-candidate) within 2 days of spike close. Otherwise spike learning evaporates.
rule.rationale: Knowledge in spiker's head decays fast; published outcome compounds.

rule.statement: Within 2 weeks: either (a) action the spike into a real story OR (b) explicitly discard with rationale. No zombie spikes.
rule.rationale: Spikes that go nowhere drain morale; explicit close keeps cycle clean.
